description_long: This is a four-story pastry shop building with a classic European facade, featuring a pale pink and cream color scheme. The ground floor has large glass windows and doors, likely for the shop entrance and display, with a greenish sign above the main entrance. The upper floors have symmetrically arranged windows with decorative lintels and subtle architectural detailing, while the roof is steeply pitched with dormer windows and two chimneys. The side and rear walls are plain and unadorned, lacking windows or decorative features. The overall structure is tall and narrow, typical of urban commercial buildings in historic city centers.
description: A tall, four-story European-style pastry shop with a decorative facade, large ground-floor windows, and a steep roof with dormers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ornate facade, large shop windows, entrance doors, and a greenish sign above the ground floor.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain, windowless wall and the building's narrow profile.
description_view_2: Rear view with a flat, unadorned wall and the sloped roof visible from above.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, also plain and windowless, mirroring the other side.
attribute_storefront_signage: A rectangular greenish sign is mounted above the main entrance on the ground floor. The sign appears to have white or light-colored text, though the exact wording is not legible. The signage is simple, horizontally oriented, and spans the width of the main entrance, with no visible logos or decorative elements beyond the text.
attribute_architectural_style: Neoclassical with European urban influences, featuring symmetrical window arrangements, decorative lintels, a steeply pitched roof with dormers, and a restrained color palette.
